---
title: Causual inference
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# 01_core_prepare_data.R
# Revised core specification for causal analysis

# 0. Packages

required_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "janitor",
  "tableone"
)

new_packages <- required_packages[!(required_packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(new_packages) > 0) install.packages(new_packages)

file_path <- file.choose()
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readxl)

credit_raw <- read_csv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names()

dim(credit_raw)
names(credit_raw)


# 2. Check columns

required_cols <- c(
  "id", "limit_bal", "sex", "education", "marriage", "age",
  "pay_0", "pay_2", "pay_3", "pay_4", "pay_5", "pay_6",
  "default_payment_next_month"
)

missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(credit_raw))
missing_cols


# 3. Clean data

credit_clean <- credit_raw %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(
    education = case_when(
      education %in% c(0, 5, 6) ~ 4L,
      TRUE ~ as.integer(education)
    ),
    marriage = case_when(
      marriage == 0 ~ 3L,
      TRUE ~ as.integer(marriage)
    ),
    default_next_month = as.integer(default_payment_next_month)
  ) %>%
  select(-id, -default_payment_next_month)

# 4. Recode factors

credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("male", "female")),
    education = factor(
      education,
      levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
      labels = c("graduate_school", "university", "high_school", "other")
    ),
    marriage = factor(
      marriage,
      levels = c(1, 2, 3),
      labels = c("married", "single", "other")
    )
  )

# 5. Create treatment

limit_cutoff_median <- median(credit_clean$limit_bal, na.rm = TRUE)
limit_cutoff_p75 <- quantile(credit_clean$limit_bal, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    high_limit = if_else(limit_bal > limit_cutoff_median, 1L, 0L),
    high_limit_p75 = if_else(limit_bal > limit_cutoff_p75, 1L, 0L)
  )


# 6. Core covariates

core_cov_demo  <- c("sex", "education", "marriage", "age")
core_cov_repay <- c("pay_0", "pay_2", "pay_3", "pay_4", "pay_5", "pay_6")

core_covariates <- c(core_cov_demo, core_cov_repay)

core_covariates
length(core_covariates)

# 7. Core analysis dataset

analysis_core <- credit_clean %>%
  select(
    default_next_month,
    high_limit,
    high_limit_p75,
    limit_bal,
    all_of(core_covariates)
  )

dim(analysis_core)
names(analysis_core)

# 8. Quick descriptive checks

cat("\n===== DATA SHAPE =====\n")
print(dim(credit_clean))
print(dim(analysis_core))

cat("\n===== DEFAULT RATE =====\n")
print(mean(credit_clean$default_next_month))

cat("\n===== TREATMENT SPLIT: MEDIAN =====\n")
print(table(credit_clean$high_limit))
print(prop.table(table(credit_clean$high_limit)))

cat("\n===== TREATMENT SPLIT: P75 =====\n")
print(table(credit_clean$high_limit_p75))
print(prop.table(table(credit_clean$high_limit_p75)))

cat("\n===== LIMIT SUMMARY BY TREATMENT =====\n")
print(tapply(credit_clean$limit_bal, credit_clean$high_limit, summary))

# 9. Baseline balance table (CORE only)
vars_for_balance_core <- c(
  "age", "sex", "education", "marriage",
  "pay_0", "pay_2", "pay_3", "pay_4", "pay_5", "pay_6"
)

factor_vars_core <- c("sex", "education", "marriage")

tab_core <- CreateTableOne(
  vars = vars_for_balance_core,
  strata = "high_limit",
  data = credit_clean,
  factorVars = factor_vars_core,
  test = TRUE
)

cat("\n===== BASELINE BALANCE TABLE: CORE =====\n")
print(tab_core, smd = TRUE)

# 10. Save files
write_csv(credit_clean, "credit_clean_revised.csv")
write_csv(analysis_core, "analysis_core.csv")

saveRDS(core_covariates, "core_covariates.rds")
saveRDS(tab_core, "tab_core_balance.rds")

cat("\n===== SAVED FILES =====\n")
cat("credit_clean_revised.csv\n")
cat("analysis_core.csv\n")
cat("core_covariates.rds\n")
cat("tab_core_balance.rds\n")
```

```{r}
# 02_core_psm_ipw.R
# Revised core PSM + IPW

# -------------------------
# 0. Packages
# -------------------------
required_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "MatchIt",
  "cobalt",
  "broom"
)

new_packages <- required_packages[!(required_packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(new_packages) > 0) install.packages(new_packages)

library(tidyverse)
library(MatchIt)
library(cobalt)
library(broom)

# -------------------------
# 1. Read prepared data
# -------------------------
credit_clean <- read_csv("credit_clean_revised.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
analysis_core <- read_csv("analysis_core.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
core_covariates <- readRDS("core_covariates.rds")

# recode factor variables after reloading csv
credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex),
    education = factor(education),
    marriage = factor(marriage)
  )

analysis_core <- analysis_core %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex),
    education = factor(education),
    marriage = factor(marriage)
  )

# -------------------------
# 2. Propensity score model (CORE)
# -------------------------
ps_formula_core <- as.formula(
  paste("high_limit ~", paste(core_covariates, collapse = " + "))
)

ps_model_core <- glm(
  ps_formula_core,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

summary(ps_model_core)

credit_clean$pscore_core <- predict(ps_model_core, type = "response")

cat("\n===== PROPENSITY SCORE SUMMARY =====\n")
print(summary(credit_clean$pscore_core))

cat("\n===== PROPENSITY SCORE BY TREATMENT =====\n")
print(tapply(credit_clean$pscore_core, credit_clean$high_limit, summary))

cat("\n===== RANGE BY TREATMENT =====\n")
print(range(credit_clean$pscore_core[credit_clean$high_limit == 0]))
print(range(credit_clean$pscore_core[credit_clean$high_limit == 1]))

# -------------------------
# 3. Plot overlap
# -------------------------
p_ps <- ggplot(credit_clean, aes(x = pscore_core, fill = factor(high_limit))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(
    title = "Propensity Score Distribution by Treatment Group (Core Specification)",
    x = "Propensity score",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_ps)
ggsave("core_pscore_distribution.png", p_ps, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# -------------------------
# 4. PSM: nearest neighbor
# -------------------------
m_core <- matchit(
  ps_formula_core,
  data = credit_clean,
  method = "nearest",
  distance = "logit",
  replace = FALSE
)

cat("\n===== MATCHING SUMMARY: NEAREST =====\n")
print(summary(m_core, standardize = TRUE))

# -------------------------
# 5. PSM: caliper matching
# -------------------------
m_core_cal <- matchit(
  ps_formula_core,
  data = credit_clean,
  method = "nearest",
  distance = "logit",
  replace = FALSE,
  caliper = 0.2
)

cat("\n===== MATCHING SUMMARY: CALIPER =====\n")
print(summary(m_core_cal, standardize = TRUE))

# Love plot
p_love <- love.plot(m_core_cal, binary = "std")
png("core_love_plot_psm_caliper.png", width = 1000, height = 800)
love.plot(m_core_cal, binary = "std")
dev.off()

# -------------------------
# 6. Matched data and ATT
# -------------------------
matched_core_cal <- match.data(m_core_cal)

cat("\n===== MATCHED SAMPLE SIZE =====\n")
print(dim(matched_core_cal))
print(table(matched_core_cal$high_limit))
print(prop.table(table(matched_core_cal$high_limit)))

cat("\n===== DEFAULT RATE IN MATCHED SAMPLE =====\n")
print(with(matched_core_cal, tapply(default_next_month, high_limit, mean)))

att_core_psm <- with(
  matched_core_cal,
  mean(default_next_month[high_limit == 1]) -
    mean(default_next_month[high_limit == 0])
)

cat("\n===== ATT FROM CORE PSM =====\n")
print(att_core_psm)

glm_core_matched <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit,
  data = matched_core_cal,
  family = binomial()
)

cat("\n===== LOGIT ON MATCHED SAMPLE =====\n")
print(summary(glm_core_matched))
cat("\n===== ODDS RATIO (MATCHED LOGIT) =====\n")
print(exp(coef(glm_core_matched)))

# -------------------------
# 7. IPW: stabilized weights
# -------------------------
p_treat <- mean(credit_clean$high_limit == 1)
p_ctrl  <- 1 - p_treat

credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sw_ate_core = if_else(
      high_limit == 1,
      p_treat / pscore_core,
      p_ctrl / (1 - pscore_core)
    )
  )

cap_sw_core <- quantile(credit_clean$sw_ate_core, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)

credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sw_ate_core_trim = pmin(sw_ate_core, cap_sw_core)
  )

cat("\n===== STABILIZED WEIGHTS SUMMARY =====\n")
print(summary(credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim))
print(quantile(
  credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim,
  probs = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.5, 0.95, 0.99),
  na.rm = TRUE
))

hist(credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim, breaks = 50)
boxplot(credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim)

# -------------------------
# 8. Balance after weighting
# -------------------------
bal_core_ipw <- bal.tab(
  high_limit ~ age + sex + education + marriage +
    pay_0 + pay_2 + pay_3 + pay_4 + pay_5 + pay_6,
  data = credit_clean,
  weights = credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim,
  method = "weighting",
  estimand = "ATE",
  un = TRUE
)

cat("\n===== BALANCE AFTER CORE IPW =====\n")
print(bal_core_ipw)

png("core_love_plot_ipw.png", width = 1000, height = 800)
love.plot(
  high_limit ~ age + sex + education + marriage +
    pay_0 + pay_2 + pay_3 + pay_4 + pay_5 + pay_6,
  data = credit_clean,
  weights = credit_clean$sw_ate_core_trim,
  method = "weighting",
  estimand = "ATE",
  binary = "std"
)
dev.off()

# -------------------------
# 9. ATE from IPW
# -------------------------
yt_core <- with(
  subset(credit_clean, high_limit == 1),
  weighted.mean(default_next_month, w = sw_ate_core_trim)
)

yc_core <- with(
  subset(credit_clean, high_limit == 0),
  weighted.mean(default_next_month, w = sw_ate_core_trim)
)

ate_core_ipw <- yt_core - yc_core

cat("\n===== CORE IPW ATE =====\n")
print(yt_core)
print(yc_core)
print(ate_core_ipw)

m_core_ipw <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial(),
  weights = sw_ate_core_trim
)

cat("\n===== WEIGHTED LOGIT (CORE IPW) =====\n")
print(summary(m_core_ipw))
cat("\n===== ODDS RATIO (CORE IPW LOGIT) =====\n")
print(exp(coef(m_core_ipw)))

# -------------------------
# 10. Save outputs
# -------------------------
write_csv(credit_clean, "credit_clean_core_ps.csv")
write_csv(matched_core_cal, "matched_core_cal.csv")

saveRDS(ps_model_core, "ps_model_core.rds")
saveRDS(m_core, "m_core.rds")
saveRDS(m_core_cal, "m_core_cal.rds")
saveRDS(glm_core_matched, "glm_core_matched.rds")
saveRDS(m_core_ipw, "m_core_ipw.rds")

core_result_summary <- data.frame(
  method = c("PSM ATT (core)", "IPW ATE (core)"),
  estimate = c(att_core_psm, ate_core_ipw)
)

write.csv(core_result_summary, "core_result_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n===== SAVED FILES =====\n")
cat("credit_clean_core_ps.csv\n")
cat("matched_core_cal.csv\n")
cat("ps_model_core.rds\n")
cat("m_core.rds\n")
cat("m_core_cal.rds\n")
cat("glm_core_matched.rds\n")
cat("m_core_ipw.rds\n")
cat("core_result_summary.csv\n")
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 03_core_causal_forest.R
# Revised core Causal Forest
# =========================================================

# -------------------------
# 0. Packages
# -------------------------
required_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "grf",
  "ggplot2"
)

new_packages <- required_packages[!(required_packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(new_packages) > 0) install.packages(new_packages)

library(tidyverse)
library(grf)
library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------
# 1. Read prepared data
# -------------------------
credit_clean <- read_csv("credit_clean_revised.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
analysis_core <- read_csv("analysis_core.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
core_covariates <- readRDS("core_covariates.rds")

# recode factor variables after reloading csv
analysis_core <- analysis_core %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex),
    education = factor(education),
    marriage = factor(marriage)
  )

# -------------------------
# 2. Prepare X, Y, W
# -------------------------
Y <- analysis_core$default_next_month
W <- analysis_core$high_limit

X <- model.matrix(
  as.formula(paste("~", paste(core_covariates, collapse = " + "))),
  data = analysis_core
)[, -1, drop = FALSE]

cat("\n===== X Y W DIMENSIONS =====\n")
print(dim(X))
print(length(Y))
print(length(W))

# -------------------------
# 3. Nuisance models
# -------------------------
y.forest <- regression_forest(X, Y)
w.forest <- regression_forest(X, W)

Y.hat <- predict(y.forest)$predictions
W.hat <- predict(w.forest)$predictions

cat("\n===== W.HAT SUMMARY BEFORE TRIMMING =====\n")
print(summary(W.hat))
print(range(W.hat))

# Trim extreme propensity estimates
W.hat <- pmin(pmax(W.hat, 0.01), 0.99)

cat("\n===== W.HAT SUMMARY AFTER TRIMMING =====\n")
print(summary(W.hat))
print(range(W.hat))

# -------------------------
# 4. Train causal forest
# -------------------------
set.seed(123)

cf_core <- causal_forest(
  X = X,
  Y = Y,
  W = W,
  Y.hat = Y.hat,
  W.hat = W.hat,
  num.trees = 2000,
  honesty = TRUE,
  seed = 123
)

# -------------------------
# 5. ATE estimates
# -------------------------
ate_core_all <- average_treatment_effect(cf_core, target.sample = "all")
ate_core_overlap <- average_treatment_effect(cf_core, target.sample = "overlap")

cat("\n===== CORE CF ATE (ALL SAMPLE) =====\n")
print(ate_core_all)

cat("\n===== CORE CF ATE (OVERLAP SAMPLE) =====\n")
print(ate_core_overlap)

# -------------------------
# 6. CATE predictions
# -------------------------
tau_hat_core <- predict(cf_core)$predictions

cat("\n===== CORE CATE SUMMARY =====\n")
print(summary(tau_hat_core))
cat("\n===== CORE MEAN CATE =====\n")
print(mean(tau_hat_core))

# attach to data
analysis_core$cate_hat <- tau_hat_core

# -------------------------
# 7. CATE distribution plots
# -------------------------
p_hist <- ggplot(analysis_core, aes(x = cate_hat)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of estimated CATE (Core Specification)",
    x = "Estimated CATE",
    y = "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_hist)
ggsave("core_cf_cate_distribution.png", p_hist, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

p_density <- ggplot(analysis_core, aes(x = cate_hat)) +
  geom_density() +
  labs(
    title = "Density of estimated CATE (Core Specification)",
    x = "Estimated CATE",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_density)
ggsave("core_cf_cate_density.png", p_density, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# -------------------------
# 8. Quartiles of CATE
# -------------------------
analysis_core <- analysis_core %>%
  mutate(
    cate_quartile = ntile(cate_hat, 4)
  )

cate_quartile_summary <- analysis_core %>%
  group_by(cate_quartile) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_cate = mean(cate_hat),
    mean_default = mean(default_next_month),
    mean_limit = mean(limit_bal),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n===== CATE QUARTILE SUMMARY =====\n")
print(cate_quartile_summary)

# -------------------------
# 9. Variable importance
# -------------------------
var_imp_core <- variable_importance(cf_core)

varimp_core_df <- data.frame(
  variable = colnames(X),
  importance = var_imp_core
) %>%
  arrange(desc(importance))

cat("\n===== TOP 15 VARIABLE IMPORTANCE =====\n")
print(head(varimp_core_df, 15))

p_varimp <- varimp_core_df %>%
  slice_max(order_by = importance, n = 15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 15 Variable Importance in Causal Forest (Core)",
    x = "Variable",
    y = "Importance"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_varimp)
ggsave("core_cf_variable_importance.png", p_varimp, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# -------------------------
# 10. Simple subgroup heterogeneity
# -------------------------
# Age groups
analysis_core <- analysis_core %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      age < 30 ~ "under_30",
      age >= 30 & age < 40 ~ "30_39",
      age >= 40 & age < 50 ~ "40_49",
      TRUE ~ "50_plus"
    )
  )

subgroup_age_core <- analysis_core %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_cate = mean(cate_hat),
    sd_cate = sd(cate_hat),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n===== SUBGROUP AGE (CORE) =====\n")
print(subgroup_age_core)

# Repayment history groups
analysis_core <- analysis_core %>%
  mutate(
    pay0_group = case_when(
      pay_0 <= 0 ~ "on_time_or_early",
      pay_0 == 1 ~ "1_month_delay",
      pay_0 >= 2 ~ "serious_delay"
    )
  )

subgroup_pay0_core <- analysis_core %>%
  group_by(pay0_group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_cate = mean(cate_hat),
    sd_cate = sd(cate_hat),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n===== SUBGROUP PAY0 (CORE) =====\n")
print(subgroup_pay0_core)

# -------------------------
# 11. Policy rule
# -------------------------
analysis_core$recommend_highlimit <- ifelse(analysis_core$cate_hat < 0, 1, 0)

cat("\n===== SIMPLE POLICY RULE =====\n")
print(table(analysis_core$recommend_highlimit))
print(prop.table(table(analysis_core$recommend_highlimit)))

# -------------------------
# 12. Save outputs
# -------------------------
write_csv(analysis_core, "analysis_core_with_cate.csv")
write.csv(cate_quartile_summary, "cate_quartile_summary_core.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(varimp_core_df, "varimp_core_df.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(subgroup_age_core, "subgroup_age_core.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(subgroup_pay0_core, "subgroup_pay0_core.csv", row.names = FALSE)

saveRDS(cf_core, "cf_core.rds")
saveRDS(y.forest, "y_forest_core.rds")
saveRDS(w.forest, "w_forest_core.rds")

cat("\n===== SAVED FILES =====\n")
cat("analysis_core_with_cate.csv\n")
cat("cate_quartile_summary_core.csv\n")
cat("varimp_core_df.csv\n")
cat("subgroup_age_core.csv\n")
cat("subgroup_pay0_core.csv\n")
cat("cf_core.rds\n")
cat("y_forest_core.rds\n")
cat("w_forest_core.rds\n")
```

```{r}
#install.packages("EValue")
library(EValue)

beta_psm <- coef(glm_core_matched)["high_limit"]
se_psm   <- summary(glm_core_matched)$coefficients["high_limit", "Std. Error"]

or_psm <- exp(beta_psm)
ci_low_psm  <- exp(beta_psm - 1.96 * se_psm)
ci_high_psm <- exp(beta_psm + 1.96 * se_psm)

or_psm
ci_low_psm
ci_high_psm

evalues.OR(
  est = or_psm,
  lo = ci_low_psm,
  hi = ci_high_psm,
  rare = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
beta_ipw <- coef(m_core_ipw)["high_limit"]
se_ipw   <- summary(m_core_ipw)$coefficients["high_limit", "Std. Error"]

or_ipw <- exp(beta_ipw)
ci_low_ipw  <- exp(beta_ipw - 1.96 * se_ipw)
ci_high_ipw <- exp(beta_ipw + 1.96 * se_ipw)

or_ipw
ci_low_ipw
ci_high_ipw

evalues.OR(
  est = or_ipw,
  lo = ci_low_ipw,
  hi = ci_high_ipw,
  rare = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

n_sim <- 500
placebo_est <- numeric(n_sim)

for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  placebo_treat <- sample(credit_clean$high_limit)
  
  m_placebo <- glm(
    default_next_month ~ placebo_treat + age + sex + education + marriage +
      pay_0 + pay_2 + pay_3 + pay_4 + pay_5 + pay_6,
    data = credit_clean,
    family = binomial()
  )
  
  placebo_est[i] <- coef(m_placebo)["placebo_treat"]
}

summary(placebo_est)

logit_core_full <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit + age + sex + education + marriage +
    pay_0 + pay_2 + pay_3 + pay_4 + pay_5 + pay_6,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial()
)

real_est <- coef(logit_core_full)["high_limit"]
real_est

hist(placebo_est, breaks = 40,
     main = "Placebo distribution of treatment estimates",
     xlab = "Placebo treatment coefficient")
abline(v = real_est, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# BENCHMARK LOGIT MODELS - CORE SPECIFICATION
# =========================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(broom)

# Đọc dữ liệu đã làm sạch
credit_clean <- readr::read_csv("credit_clean_revised.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# recode lại factor nếu cần
credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex),
    education = factor(education),
    marriage = factor(marriage)
  )

# -------------------------
# Logit 1: chỉ treatment
# -------------------------
logit_1 <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial()
)

# -------------------------
# Logit 2: thêm demographic covariates
# -------------------------
logit_2 <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit + age + sex + education + marriage,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial()
)

# -------------------------
# Logit 3: core benchmark model
# demographic + repayment history
# -------------------------
logit_3 <- glm(
  default_next_month ~ high_limit + age + sex + education + marriage +
    pay_0 + pay_2 + pay_3 + pay_4 + pay_5 + pay_6,
  data = credit_clean,
  family = binomial()
)

# -------------------------
# Xem summary từng mô hình
# -------------------------
summary(logit_1)
summary(logit_2)
summary(logit_3)

# -------------------------
# Trích riêng hệ số high_limit
# -------------------------
benchmark_results <- bind_rows(
  tidy(logit_1) %>% mutate(model = "Logit 1"),
  tidy(logit_2) %>% mutate(model = "Logit 2"),
  tidy(logit_3) %>% mutate(model = "Logit 3")
) %>%
  filter(term == "high_limit") %>%
  mutate(
    odds_ratio = exp(estimate),
    conf.low.or = exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),
    conf.high.or = exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error)
  ) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value,
         odds_ratio, conf.low.or, conf.high.or)

benchmark_results
library(ggplot2)

benchmark_plot <- benchmark_results %>%
  mutate(
    conf.low = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error
  )

ggplot(benchmark_plot, aes(x = model, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Benchmark logit estimates for high_limit",
    x = "Model",
    y = "Coefficient of high_limit"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# BEFORE vs AFTER MATCHING TABLE (CORE SPECIFICATION)
# =========================================================

# Nếu chưa cài:
# install.packages(c("tidyverse", "MatchIt"))

library(tidyverse)
library(MatchIt)

# -------------------------
# 1. Đọc dữ liệu
# -------------------------
credit_clean <- readr::read_csv("credit_clean_revised.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# recode factor variables
credit_clean <- credit_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex),
    education = factor(education),
    marriage = factor(marriage)
  )

# -------------------------
# 2. Tạo công thức propensity score core
# -------------------------
core_covariates <- c(
  "sex", "education", "marriage", "age",
  "pay_0", "pay_2", "pay_3", "pay_4", "pay_5", "pay_6"
)

ps_formula_core <- as.formula(
  paste("high_limit ~", paste(core_covariates, collapse = " + "))
)

# -------------------------
# 3. Chạy caliper matching
# -------------------------
m_core_cal <- matchit(
  ps_formula_core,
  data = credit_clean,
  method = "nearest",
  distance = "logit",
  replace = FALSE,
  caliper = 0.2
)

# -------------------------
# 4. Lấy summary matching
# -------------------------
sum_core_cal <- summary(m_core_cal, standardize = TRUE)

# -------------------------
# 5. Tạo bảng before-after SMD
# -------------------------
before_after_smd <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(sum_core_cal$sum.all),
  SMD_Before = sum_core_cal$sum.all[, "Std. Mean Diff."],
  SMD_After  = sum_core_cal$sum.matched[, "Std. Mean Diff."]
) %>%
  mutate(
    SMD_Before = round(SMD_Before, 4),
    SMD_After  = round(SMD_After, 4),
    Improvement = round(abs(SMD_Before) - abs(SMD_After), 4)
  )

before_after_smd
before_after_focus <- before_after_smd %>%
  dplyr::filter(Variable %in% c(
    "age", "pay_0", "pay_2", "pay_3", "pay_4", "pay_5", "pay_6",
    "sexfemale", "educationgraduate_school", "educationuniversity",
    "educationhigh_school", "marriagemarried", "marriagesingle"
  ))

before_after_focus
```
